ab: ana misu tqbaqun cikay?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: uya, smwan cu.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: ana misu tqbaqun cikay?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: iyat cu, ini kpiyux qbaqun mu.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: maki iyaw su qlyan qani ga? smoya cu musa mkan isu, pswan su?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: uya, naw misu muyax.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: maki iyaw su cyugan ga? akya misu tqbaqun iyaw Tayal cyunga.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於泰雅族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Atayal.

ab: musa cu rgyax cyugan, ini cu kaki muyax ay!
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: pnung mu ga, baqun su calay qabax iyaw na Tayal, smoya cu tqbaq cikay isu.
ch: A:我聽說您對泰雅族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Atayal, I would like to ask you a question.

ab: ini kpiyux ciyan qbaqun mu, ana ga tlamay mu kmayan.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: ana misu psingun cikay? maha cqani ga baqun mu mmiru babaw nya. la.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: uya ga, psingi cu.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pisa kawas su la?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: qesu mpux lpgan spat kawas mu la.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: sening su calay na.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ini ga, tyaba kawas mu la.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: minkahol su inu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: knahol cu qalang Skikun.
en: B: I’m from [Four Seasons Village]. *Please change it to an ethnic location.

ab: Tayal kneril su uyi?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是泰雅族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Atayal?

ab: iyat, Amis hiya.
en: B: No, she’s an Ami.

ab: nanu na ke ga baqun su kmayan?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: baq cu kmayan ke na Tayal ru Truku.
ch: B: 我會說泰雅語和太魯閣語。
en: B: I can speak Atayal and Telugu.

ab: mqas cu calay mwah tqbaq isu, lani pinquyu cikay gaga na qmalup qani ga
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: uya pqiyay misu, garin cu sening na ga, si cu usa qmalup rgyax kryax.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: mhway su calay, talagay kinblaq pinqiyan su coni qani.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: cbalay pinqiyan mu qani ay, snbilan ni bnheci myan cyunga.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: ski cqani tnbaqan misu la. mhway calay pinsbaq su qani.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: ini, pkayan ta babaw nya loyi.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: musa cu la, akya cu smoya mung piyux hayi iyaw qnxan cyunga.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: uya, thway musa su ki.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
